findings.description: Django's DEBUG=True exposes full stack traces, local variables, and settings to any user who triggers an error. Must be False in production.
findings.recommendation: Set DEBUG = False for all production deployments.

findings.description: A literal string value is used as Django's SECRET_KEY. If this file is committed to version control the secret is exposed. SECRET_KEY must be loaded from an environment variable or secrets manager.
findings.recommendation: Load SECRET_KEY from the environment: SECRET_KEY = os.environ['DJANGO_SECRET_KEY']

findings.description: ALLOWED_HOSTS = [] is only safe while DEBUG=True. In production with DEBUG=False it rejects all requests and is a sign the value has not been configured for deployment.
findings.recommendation: Set ALLOWED_HOSTS to your explicit domain list before deploying.

findings.description: SESSION_COOKIE_SECURE is not present in any settings file. Django defaults to False, which allows the session cookie to be transmitted over plain HTTP, enabling session hijacking.
findings.recommendation: Add SESSION_COOKIE_SECURE = True to your production settings.
